answers: Self-confidence is believing in your own abilities and worth. Build it by setting small goals and achieving them, practicing self-compassion, and stepping out of your comfort zone gradually.
Confidence barate choto choto goal set koro ar achieve koro, positive self-talk practice koro, nijer achievements celebrate koro, ar nijer sathe compare koro — onno karo sathe na.
Lack of confidence often comes from past experiences, negative self-talk, or comparing yourself to others. The good news is confidence is a skill you can build with consistent practice.

Believe in yourself by acknowledging your strengths, learning from failures instead of fearing them, surrounding yourself with supportive people, and reminding yourself that everyone starts somewhere.
